# Feature Specification: M0.2 -- Multiplayer Sync
*Generated by Game Studio Sub-Agents v1.0*

## Feature Overview
**Feature Name**: Real-Time Multiplayer Position Synchronization
**Project**: Nookstead
**Engine**: Phaser.js 3 + Next.js 16 + Colyseus 0.15+
**Priority**: High (Critical Path -- validates MMO foundation)
**Estimated Effort**: 2 weeks (10 working days)
**Dependencies**: M0.1 (Walking on Tiles)
**Owner**: Mechanics Developer
**Data Impact**: player_movement_sample, zone_transition, performance_sample (WebSocket latency)

## Market Justification
**Player Demand**: Nookstead is an MMO. Multiplayer is not optional -- it is the product category. Players must see each other moving in real-time.
**Competitive Analysis**: Palia supports 25 concurrent players in a zone. Animal Crossing supports 8 visitors. Stardew supports 4 co-op. Nookstead targets 100 players per server.
**Market Impact**: Multiplayer creates the "living world" feeling that is central to the MMO life sim promise. Without it, Nookstead is just a single-player farming game.
**Priority Rationale**: Second milestone because it validates the technical foundation (WebSocket, Colyseus) that all future multiplayer features depend on.

## Problem Statement
Multiple players must connect to a shared game world and see each other's characters moving in real-time. NPC entities must also be visible to all players with server-authoritative positions. The server must be the source of truth for all entity positions to prevent cheating.

## User Stories
- **As a player**, I want to **see other players walking around the town** so that **the world feels alive and social**
- **As a player**, I want to **see NPC characters walking on their schedules** so that **the town feels inhabited**
- **As a player**, I want **smooth movement of other characters** so that **the experience does not feel laggy or broken**

## Acceptance Criteria
### Must Have
- [ ] Colyseus game server running with TownRoom
- [ ] Players connect via WebSocket from browser client
- [ ] Each player sees all other players' sprites and movements in real-time
- [ ] Player join/leave is visible (sprite appears/disappears)
- [ ] NPC entities have server-defined positions visible to all clients
- [ ] Server is authoritative (client sends input, server validates and broadcasts positions)
- [ ] Position updates at 10 ticks/sec (100ms interval)
- [ ] Latency < 150ms for position updates
- [ ] Works with 10 concurrent players without performance degradation

### Should Have
- [ ] Client-side interpolation for smooth movement between server ticks
- [ ] Player name labels above sprites
- [ ] Basic NPC pathfinding (A* on collision grid) with server-calculated movement
- [ ] Graceful reconnection on WebSocket disconnect

### Could Have
- [ ] Player count display in HUD
- [ ] NPC walk animations (not just position updates)
- [ ] Area-of-interest filtering (only sync entities near the player)

## Functional Requirements
### Network Architecture
```
Client (Phaser.js)              Colyseus Server
  |                                  |
  |-- JOIN room "town-1" ---------->|
  |<--- Room state (all entities) --|
  |                                  |
  |-- Input: move(direction) ------>|  (10x/sec max)
  |<--- State patch (positions) ----|  (10x/sec broadcast)
  |                                  |
  |-- LEAVE ----------------------->|
```

### State Synchronization
- Server maintains `WorldState` schema with all player and NPC positions
- Client sends movement input (direction + speed), NOT position
- Server validates movement against collision map and updates state
- Colyseus patches state to all clients at `patchRate: 100ms` (10/sec)
- Client-side interpolation smooths movement between patches

### Data Structures
```typescript
// Client -> Server message
interface MoveInput {
  direction: { x: number; y: number };  // Normalized (-1 to 1)
  sequence: number;  // For client-side prediction
}

// Server WorldState (Colyseus Schema) -- see engine-setup.md for full schema
// Players: MapSchema<PlayerState>
// NPCs: MapSchema<NPCState>
// Clock: GameClockState
```

## Technical Requirements
### Performance Constraints
- **Network Bandwidth**: < 10 KB/sec per client for position sync (10 updates/sec, ~100 bytes each)
- **Server CPU**: Handle 100 clients + 50 NPCs tick processing in < 50ms per tick
- **Client CPU**: Interpolation + sprite updates for 150 entities in < 5ms per frame

### Integration Points
- **Systems Used**: Colyseus.js client SDK, Colyseus Room, Phaser sprite management
- **Events Triggered**: `player_joined(id, name)`, `player_left(id)`, `npc_position_updated(id, x, y)`
- **Events Consumed**: `player_moved(direction)` from input system (M0.1)

## Balancing Parameters
- **Tick Rate**: 10/sec - Range: 5-20 - Purpose: Balance between responsiveness and bandwidth
- **Interpolation Duration**: 100ms - Range: 50-200 - Purpose: Smoothness vs responsiveness tradeoff
- **Max Players Per Room**: 100 - Range: 20-200 - Purpose: Server capacity planning

## Edge Cases & Error Handling
- **WebSocket disconnect**: Client shows "Reconnecting..." overlay; attempts reconnect 3 times at 2s intervals; on failure, returns to lobby
- **High latency (>300ms)**: Show latency warning icon in HUD; increase interpolation buffer
- **Server full (100 players)**: Show "Server Full" message with option to join queue or pick another server
- **Player position desync**: Server periodically sends full state correction; client snaps to corrected position

## Analytics Integration
### Telemetry Events
- **performance_sample**: Extended with `websocket_latency_ms` and `active_players_in_room`
- **player_movement_sample**: Now server-verified positions (more accurate heatmaps)
- **New event: server_room_stats**: Every 60 seconds -- player_count, npc_count, tick_processing_time_ms

## Testing Plan
### Unit Tests
- [ ] Colyseus room creates with correct initial state
- [ ] Player join adds PlayerState to WorldState
- [ ] Player movement input updates position correctly (server-side)
- [ ] Player leave removes PlayerState
- [ ] NPC positions update according to schedule

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Two clients connect and see each other
- [ ] Client A's movement is visible on Client B within 200ms
- [ ] NPC positions are consistent across all clients
- [ ] Client reconnects after disconnect and syncs to current state

### Performance Tests
- [ ] 10 concurrent clients: latency < 150ms, server tick < 50ms
- [ ] 50 concurrent clients: latency < 200ms, server tick < 80ms
- [ ] Bandwidth per client < 10 KB/sec

## Implementation Notes
### Development Approach
1. Generate apps/server Nx project with Colyseus (Day 1-2)
2. Implement WorldState schema with PlayerState (Day 2-3)
3. Implement TownRoom with join/leave/movement (Day 3-4)
4. Implement Colyseus.js client connection in game (Day 4-5)
5. Render other players as Phaser sprites (Day 5-6)
6. Client-side interpolation (Day 6-7)
7. Add NPC entities to server state (Day 7-8)
8. NPC basic pathfinding (A* on collision grid) (Day 8-9)
9. Testing and optimization (Day 9-10)
